title: VPN Protocol Comparison 2026: OpenVPN vs WireGuard vs IKEv2
description: Which VPN protocol should you use? We compare OpenVPN, WireGuard, and IKEv2 on speed, security, and compatibility to help you pick the right one.
content:
## Why VPN Protocols Matter

The protocol is the foundation of your VPN connection. It determines speed, security level, how well the connection holds up on unstable networks, and whether it can pass through restrictive firewalls. Most VPN apps default to automatic protocol selection. Knowing the differences helps you override that when the default is wrong for your situation.

## WireGuard: Fastest and Most Modern

WireGuard is the best choice for most users in 2026. It is significantly faster than OpenVPN, reconnects instantly when your network switches (cellular to WiFi), and has a much smaller codebase (around 4,000 lines vs 70,000+ for OpenVPN), which means a smaller attack surface. The main trade-off is that WireGuard requires storing your IP address on the server for the duration of the connection session, which is a minor privacy consideration compared to no-log VPN providers resolve with dynamic IP assignment.

## OpenVPN: Most Proven

OpenVPN is the most audited and widely supported protocol. It is slower than WireGuard but extremely reliable and configurable. OpenVPN over TCP port 443 is the best protocol for bypassing restrictive firewalls (it looks like standard HTTPS traffic). If you need to use a VPN in a country with heavy internet filtering, OpenVPN TCP is often the most reliable option. Use OpenVPN when you need maximum firewall bypass capability or when WireGuard is not available on your VPN provider.

## IKEv2/IPSec: Best for Mobile

IKEv2 handles network switching exceptionally well. If you frequently move between WiFi and cellular, IKEv2 maintains the VPN connection during the transition without drops. It is faster than OpenVPN and built into iOS and macOS natively. The trade-off is that IKEv2 uses port 500/4500 UDP, which is blocked in some restrictive environments.

## Which to Choose

Default (most situations): WireGuard. Restrictive firewall or censorship environment: OpenVPN TCP. Mobile use with frequent network changes: IKEv2. Legacy compatibility: OpenVPN UDP.
